evals.prompt: I have a folder of local PDF invoices and I want to extract the customer name, invoice date, total amount, and line items from each one using Azure AI Content Understanding. Show me a Python script that loops through a folder, analyzes each PDF, and prints the extracted fields.
evals.expected_output: Python script that: (1) creates ContentUnderstandingClient with AzureKeyCredential or DefaultAzureCredential, (2) loops through PDF files in a directory, (3) uses begin_analyze_binary with prebuilt-invoice analyzer, (4) calls poller.result() to get the AnalysisResult, (5) accesses result.contents[0].fields for CustomerName, InvoiceDate, TotalAmount, and LineItems, (6) handles the array field LineItems by iterating item.value
assertions: Code calls poller.result() to get the result
Code accesses result.contents[0].fields to get document fields
Code handles the CustomerName, InvoiceDate, TotalAmount fields with .value
Code iterates over LineItems array field using item.value to get sub-fields
Code reads files with open() in binary mode ('rb')

evals.prompt: I want to transcribe an audio file (meeting_recording.mp3) and get a speaker-attributed transcript with timestamps. The file is stored locally. I also want to print a one-paragraph summary of the meeting. Use Azure AI Content Understanding.
evals.expected_output: Python code that: (1) creates ContentUnderstandingClient, (2) reads the MP3 file in binary mode, (3) calls begin_analyze_binary with prebuilt-audioSearch, (4) calls poller.result() and accesses result.contents[0] as AudioVisualContent, (5) iterates transcript_phrases with speaker, start_time_ms, end_time_ms, and text, (6) retrieves the Summary field from content.fields
assertions: Code reads the MP3 file in binary mode with open('meeting_recording.mp3', 'rb')
Code accesses result.contents[0] for the AudioVisualContent
Code accesses phrase.speaker, phrase.start_time_ms, phrase.end_time_ms, and phrase.text
Code retrieves the Summary field via content.fields.get('Summary')

evals.prompt: I need to build a pipeline that processes video files uploaded to Azure Blob Storage. For each video URL, I want to extract a per-segment description, total duration, and download keyframe images. Use the async Content Understanding client to process 3 videos in parallel.
evals.expected_output: Async Python code that: (1) imports ContentUnderstandingClient from azure.ai.contentunderstanding.aio, (2) uses asyncio.gather to analyze 3 URLs in parallel with begin_analyze using prebuilt-videoSearch, (3) iterates result.contents for segments with start_time_ms, end_time_ms, and markdown, (4) uses client.get_result_file with the operation ID (result.id) and 'keyframes.zip' to retrieve keyframe images
assertions: Code imports ContentUnderstandingClient from azure.ai.contentunderstanding.aio (async client)
Code uses asyncio.gather to run multiple begin_analyze calls in parallel
Code uses begin_analyze with analyzer_id='prebuilt-videoSearch' and AnalysisInput(url=...)
Code awaits poller.result() to get each AnalysisResult
Code iterates result.contents for multiple segments with start_time_ms and end_time_ms
Code calls client.get_result_file with operation_id=result.id and result_file_name='keyframes.zip'
